question: At fixed degree, do graph spectral properties predict perturbation recovery in a local vector-state graph CA across state dimension, coupling, fresh graph instances, and scale?
experimental_unit: one independently seeded regular graph plus vector-state episode

hypotheses.prediction: Degree-preserving rewired and random-regular graphs have larger normalized-Laplacian spectral gaps and lower final perturbation ratios than the ring in at least 80% of paired units.
hypotheses.falsifier: Either topology fails the spectral-gap direction, has nonnegative mean recovery delta, or is faster in fewer than 80% of paired units.

hypotheses.prediction: Adding spectral gap to coupling and log2 state dimension improves holdout RMSE for log10 final perturbation ratio by at least 5%.
hypotheses.falsifier: Relative holdout RMSE improvement is below 5% or reverses.

model.update_order: Synchronous shared nonlinear channel reaction followed by neighbor-mean coupling on the pre-update graph state.
model.rng: NetworkX graph generation and numpy initial state are deterministic functions of the episode seed; updates are deterministic.

seed_plan.pairing: Shared numeric seeds pair initial node-state streams and graph-family construction inputs across topology arms; topology changes adjacency and therefore subsequent trajectories.

exposure.eligibility: Every connected exact-degree graph contains the registered intervention node and channel.
exposure.activation: The perturbation is applied once before synchronous evolution.
exposure.state_change: The initial paired-state difference norm and all subsequent perturbation norms are retained.

analysis.predictive_baseline: Linear least-squares model using intercept, coupling, and log2 state dimension; spectral model adds normalized-Laplacian spectral gap.
analysis.uncertainty: Paired episode deltas and deterministic episode-level bootstrap 95% intervals; holdout prediction is evaluated on unseen seeds, graph instances, and node count.
analysis.multiple_comparisons: Two registered topology contrasts share one directional 80% gate; no post-hoc topology selection.
ood_conditions: holdout node count 160 versus training counts 96 and 128
analysis.stopping_rule: Stop after all declared episodes, at 1200 wall seconds, above 2048 MB RSS, above 250 MB artifacts, on graph invariant failure, or on failed determinism.

budget.execution: Sequential local CPU execution with one graph-state world resident at a time.
